GitCrisp

A clean, focused Git GUI

Visual commit graph, per-hunk staging, and streamlined remote operations — built with Python and Qt.

Download View on GitHub

Features

📊

Visual Commit Graph

Lane-based graph showing branching and merging history with lazy pagination and branch navigation.

🧩

Per-Hunk Staging

Stage or unstage individual diff hunks within a file. See exactly what goes into each commit.

📦

Stash Management

One-click stash with confirmation, view stash contents inline, pop, apply, or drop from context menu.

🌐

Remote Operations

Push, pull, and fetch run in background threads with real-time status logging. No UI freezes.

📂

Multi-Repository

Switch between repositories with persistent open and recent lists. Clone new repos from URL.

Architecture

GitCrisp follows Clean Architecture with strict layer separation and protocol-based dependency injection.

Domain Entities & Protocols
Application Commands & Queries
Infrastructure pygit2 Adapter & Repo Store
Presentation Qt Widgets & Signal Bus

Design Philosophy

Some Git operations are intentionally left out. This is not a limitation — it is a design choice.

No Merge Button

Branch merging in a team workflow is best done through pull requests, where code review, CI checks, and approval gates provide a safer and more traceable process.

No Rebase or Cherry-Pick

These operations frequently involve merge conflicts that require manual resolution. No GUI can match the flexibility of a CLI paired with your preferred editor.
